steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Transfer Resource Budget · Theory Transfer Resource Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7305 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload workers, part buffers, abort ownership, and cleanup policy; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound aggregate Upload queueSize workers, part buffers, active source streams, sockets, progress listeners, AbortControllers, descriptors, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers.
